item.title: I have started working on a WASP-HS project (Synthetic Data)
item.description:
I have recently joined on a WASP-HS research project as part of my postdoctoral research titled “Synthetic Data: Facts, Representations, and Transparency.” In this interdisciplinary project, I will explore and implement different measures for evaluating synthetic data, while also developing novel models to mitigate bias in synthetic data and representation learning.

More details about the project can be found in:

item.title: I am co-organizing a Workshop - The Politics of Model
item.description:
I am happy to share that I am co-organizing the Workshop “The Politics of Models - Bias, Representation & Transparency in Generative Models”, taking place on 25-27 August 2026 at Rånäs Slott, Sweden.

The workshop is part of WASP and WASP-HS and will bring together researchers working on questions related to bias, representation, transparency, accountability, and the broader impact of generative AI.

Researchers from WASP and WASP-HS are welcome to apply. The deadline for expression of interest is 17 April 2026, and the workshop is limited to 20 participants.

I am looking forward to the Conversations and ideas that will come out of it.

Organizers

  • Kivanç Tatar, Chalmers University of Technology
  • Kathlén Kohn, KTH Royal Institute of Technology
  • Md Fahim Sikder, Linköping University
  • Francis Lee, Södertörn University
  • Bodil Formark, Södertörn University

More details:

item.title: I am co-organizing a Hackathon
item.description:
One of the most rewarding parts of working on synthetic data is seeing it spark genuine curiosity beyond the technical community. I am proud to be co-organizing “Humanities Hackathin - AI Generated Texts and Images”, which brings humanists and AI researchers into the same room to ask hard questions about how synthetic data can and should be used in historical and literary research.

Join us in Uppsala on May 25.

Registration closes May 15.

More details:
